tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

---------

Co-authored-by: Mike Harder <mharder@microsoft.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-09T05:17:28Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-09T05:17:28Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Refactor GetInputItemsAsync to return Item and add GetInputTextAsync convenience (#57974)

* Refactor GetInputItemsAsync to return Item, add GetInputTextAsync convenience

- Change ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync() return type from
  IReadOnlyList<OutputItem> to IReadOnlyList<Item> with optional
  bool resolveReferences parameter (defaults to true)
- Add ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync() convenience method
- Add ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable<Item>) public extension
- Add ItemConversion.ToItem() reverse conversion (OutputItem → Item)
  via ModelReaderWriter JSON round-trip
- Implement dual caching in ResponseContextImpl (resolved vs unresolved)
- Add internal GetInputItemsForPersistenceAsync() for orchestrator use
- Make GetInputText(this CreateResponse) internal; keep GetInputExpanded public
- Update all 14 snippet files to use context.GetInputTextAsync() /
  context.GetInputItemsAsync() instead of request.GetInputText()
- Update handler-implementation-guide.md with new API patterns
- Add 4 multi-turn item reference E2E protocol tests
- Add 6 new ResponseContextImpl unit tests (resolveReferences, text, persistence)
- Update API listings via Export-API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Document convenience generators in README and expand CHANGELOG

- Add ResponseEventStream convenience generators section to README
  between TextResponse and full builder control (with compiled snippet)
- Add convenience generators table (OutputItemMessage, OutputItemFunctionCall,
  OutputItemReasoningItem) with links to detailed samples
- Expand CHANGELOG with comprehensive first-release feature entries covering
  all public APIs: ResponseHandler, TextResponse, ResponseEventStream,
  convenience generators, ResponseContext, GetInputItemsAsync/GetInputTextAsync,
  AgentHostBuilder, header validation, conversation ID round-trip, and tracing

Closes #57926

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

resource.text:
# Release History

## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2026-04-09)

### Features Added

- Initial beta release of new Azure.Provisioning.MySql.

# Azure Provisioning MySql client library for .NET

Azure.Provisioning.MySql simplifies declarative resource provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers in .NET.

## Getting started

### Install the package

Install the client library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):

```dotnetcli
dotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.MySql --prerelease
```

### Prerequisites

> You must have an [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/).

### Authenticate the Client

## Key concepts

This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use azd to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.

## Examples

## Troubleshooting

-   File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).
-   Check [previous questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/azure+.net) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags.

## Next steps

## Contributing

For details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing
guide][cg].

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions
require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring
that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use
your contribution. For details, visit <https://cla.microsoft.com>.

When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine
whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately
(for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the
bot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories
using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For
more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact
<opencode@microsoft.com> with any other questions or comments.

<!-- LINKS -->
[cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md
[coc]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/
[coc_faq]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** PR #57773 by @ArcturusZhang ## What Changed PR #57773 introduced the brand-new `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (commit `[REDACTED]`), providing declarative provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers. The package includes 10+ resource types: `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`, `AdvancedThreatProtection`, and more. ## Gaps Found - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** — the Getting Started section has a heading but no content explaining how to authenticate - **"Key concepts" section is skeletal** — only one generic sentence; none of the key resource types (`MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, etc.) are listed or explained - **"Examples" section is completely empty** — no code samples showing how to provision a MySQL flexible server, create a database, or configure a firewall rule - **"Next steps" section is completely empty** — no links to Azure documentation, related packages, or samples - **No sample test files exist** — `tests/Samples/` is empty so there are no snippet-backed examples to auto-generate
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` is a provisioning library — it generates Bicep/ARM templates for deployment via `azd` or the Azure CLI. No runtime credentials are required to define infrastructure. Authentication is only needed when deploying the generated templates, which is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview). ``` Replace the skeletal `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` allows you to define Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server infrastructure declaratively in .NET. The library generates Bicep templates that can be deployed using `azd` or the Azure CLI. Key resource types available in this library: | Resource Type | Description | |---|---| | `MySqlFlexibleServer` | The primary MySQL Flexible Server resource | | `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | A database hosted on the flexible server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | IP firewall rules for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | Server parameter configurations | | `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | Azure Active Directory administrator for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` | Backup resources for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | Scheduled maintenance windows | | `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | Private endpoint connections | | `AdvancedThreatProtection` | Advanced threat protection settings | For a full conceptual overview of the provisioning library pattern, see the [Azure.Provisioning README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md). ``` Replace the empty `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ```C# Snippet:Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer ``` ``` (The actual code content will be injected by `Update-Snippets.ps1` once the sample test file is created in Step 2.) Replace the empty `## Next steps` section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/mysql/flexible-server/overview) - [Azure Developer CLI (azd) documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview) - [Azure.Provisioning library README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [NuGet package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Provisioning.MySql) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples; public class Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [IgnoreOnCoverage] public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer // Create a new infrastructure definition var infrastructure = new Infrastructure("mysqlDemo"); // Define a MySQL Flexible Server var server = new MySqlFlexibleServer("mysqlserver") { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, AdministratorLogin = "adminUser", AdministratorLoginPassword = new BicepSecretValue("adminPassword"), Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled } }; infrastructure.Add(server); // Add a database to the server var database = new MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase("mydb") { Parent = server, Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infrastructure.Add(database); // Add a firewall rule to allow Azure services var firewallRule = new MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule("allowAzureServices") { Parent = server, StartIPAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIPAddress = "0.0.0.0" }; infrastructure.Add(firewallRule); #endregion } } ``` **README location:** Insert the snippet placeholder under `## Examples` / `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`. **SnippetName:** `Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer` ### Step 3: Verify README structure All required sections exist in `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` but the following are missing content: 1. ✅ Getting started / Install the package — present 2. ✅ Getting started / Prerequisites — present 3. ❌ Getting started / Authenticate the client — **empty, needs content** (see Step 1) 4. ❌ Key concepts — **skeleton only, needs expansion** (see Step 1) 5. ❌ Examples — **empty, needs snippet placeholder** (see Steps 1 and 2) 6. ✅ Troubleshooting — present 7. ❌ Next steps — **empty, needs links** (see Step 1) 8. ✅ Contributing — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects snippet code from the test file into README.md placeholders; verify README was updated with `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — regenerates API listing files if any public types were changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` — ensures consistent formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [MySql] Docs: Complete README for new Azure.Provisioning.MySql package
